items.headline: The Retiree Who Chose $65,000 Instead of $120,000 and Ended Up Richer
items.summary: Two retirees walk into retirement with the same amount of money. One builds a portfolio that pays $120,000 a year but barely grows. The other settles for $65,000 a year and focuses on dividend growth. At first glance, the first retiree appears to have won. Twenty-five years later, the second retiree may have collected more ... The Retiree Who Chose $65,000 Instead of $120,000 and Ended Up Richer

items.headline: The 15% Yield ETF That Steals Warren Buffett’s Playbook
items.summary: Buffett famously refuses to pay a dividend on Berkshire Hathaway because he can compound your cash better than you can. The VistaShares Target 15 Berkshire Select Income ETF (NYSEARCA:OMAH) disagrees, politely. OMAH holds a Berkshire-style basket of value names and overlays an options strategy designed to push out a 15% annual distribution, paid monthly. You ... The 15% Yield ETF That Steals Warren Buffett’s Playbook

items.headline: After Warren Buffett’s Successor’s Q1 Purge, Just 4 Stocks Make Up Over 50% of Berkshire Hathaway
items.summary: Warren Buffett stepped down as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE: BRK-B) on December 31, 2025, after six decades leading the conglomerate he transformed from a struggling textile mill into a $1 trillion empire. The “Oracle of Omaha” left his successor, Greg Abel, with a very concentrated portfolio: 70% of Berkshire’s $381 billion portfolio is invested in ... After Warren Buffett’s Successor’s Q1 Purge, Just 4 Stocks Make Up Over 50% of Berkshire Hathaway

items.headline: McDonald's Red Bull Drinks Test Brand Perception And Valuation Gap
items.summary: McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) is expanding its beverage lineup with new custom sodas, refreshers, and energy drinks featuring Red Bull. The move adds to its long running Coca-Cola partnership by introducing branded energy options alongside its traditional soft drinks. The refreshed beverage menu aims to capture more spending from guests who want broader choices, including energy drinks. For investors tracking NYSE:MCD, this beverage expansion comes with the stock around $284.81 and a mixed recent...
